topic: Someone discovers their mother kept every school assignment
raw_output:
[story 1]

The cardboard box smelled faintly of old pencil shavings and dried glue. Liam lifted the lid, and a cascade of neatly folded papers tumbled out. Math homework from every grade, history essays, even those little creative writing prompts from kindergarten. His breath hitched. He picked up a pristine, A-plus-graded report on the Roman Empire, the ink sharp and flawless. A tightness coiled in his chest, a heavy, unfamiliar pressure. He remembered his own desk, a chaotic landscape of crumpled worksheets and forgotten notes. He stared at the collection, a strange hollowness spreading through him. "How... how did you keep all of this?" he managed, his voice thin. The sheer volume of achievement surrounding him felt like a physical weight pressing him down.

[story 2]

Sarah traced the edge of a perfectly preserved science project, the glitter still catching the light. It was a complex model of the solar system, meticulously labeled. Her fingers trembled slightly as she ran them over the smooth plastic. She looked around her cluttered room, at the stack of incomplete assignments gathering dust on her bedside table. A sharp, unpleasant sensation prickled behind her eyes. She picked up a test paper from last semester, the answers filled in with confident, correct strokes. A slow, simmering resentment began to bubble up, hot and uncomfortable. She squeezed her eyes shut, trying to push the feeling away, but the image of her mother’s organized hoard remained burned behind her eyelids.

[story 3]

Mark found the collection tucked away in the attic, a forgotten trove of scholastic triumphs. He pulled out a stack of essays—his own, from years ago, looking embarrassingly clumsy next to the polished prose he now held. A bitter taste coated his tongue. He flipped through the pages, noticing the consistent quality, the unwavering attention to detail. A sudden wave of inadequacy washed over him, making his shoulders slump. He let out a quiet, frustrated sigh, the sound ragged in the dusty silence. He felt a deep ache in his stomach, a desire to possess that effortless competence, that effortless success his mother had cultivated.
